title: ustr2uf
text: ustr2uf Copy string data from a program buffer to a basic parameter or entity field. Explain this code long ustr2uf (UHACT hOpr, int seqNr, UCHAR* string); Copy Parameters hOpr The handle to the operation or entity parameter seqNr The sequence number of the parameter or entity field (sequence numbers start with 1) string The data to be copied to the parameter or entity field Return Values For more information, see the "Call-In API Return Codes" topic. Use Library: ucall Description ustr2uf copies the supplied string data to a basic parameter or entity field. A conversion error will result if the target parameter or field is not compatible with the data you are trying to put into it. ustr2uf converts the string from external format to Uniface format. If you have data that is already in Uniface format, use uprmputs rather than ustr2uf . Example: uuf2str, unifree, and ustr2uf The following code calls uuf2str to retrieve string data from an output parameter of one service. It then modifies the string and calls ustr2uf to pass it as an input parameter to another service. Note that, because we want to access and modify the string, we cannot use the detach/attach mechanism here. The operations OP1 and OP2 are both located in the service TESTSVC, and are defined as follows: Explain this code operation op1 params string msg : inout; endparams msg = "the quick brow fox jumps over the lazy dog" end operation op2 params string msg : in; endparams putmess msg end Copy And here is the 3GL routine: Explain this code #include <h3gl.h> #include <ctype.h> void get_and_put () { UHACT hEnv, hIn; /* Environment and instance handle */ UHACT hOp1, hOp2; /* Operation handles for OP1 amd OP2 */ long actStatus, procErr; /* Activation status and procerr */ UCHAR* pMsg; /* Pointer to string parameter data */ UCHAR* p; /* Work pointer */ ueopen(&hEnv); /* Get environment handle */ /* Instantiate service TESTSVC */ uinstnew (hEnv, (UCHAR *)"TESTSVC", 0, 0, UDEFAULT_COMM_MODE, 0, &hIn, &procErr); uinstopr (hIn, (UCHAR *)"OP1", 0, &hOp1); /* Instantiate operation OP1 */ uinstopr (hIn, (UCHAR *)"OP2", 0, &hOp2); /* Instantiate operation OP1 */ uopract (hOp1, &actStatus, &procErr); /* Activate operation OP1 */ uuf2str(hOp1, 1, &pMsg); /* Get a pointer to the string */ for ( p=pMsg; *p; p++ ) *p = toupper(*p);/* Convert string to uppercase*/ ustr2uf(hOp2, 1, pMsg); /* Copy string to OP2 input parameter */ uopract (hOp2, &actStatus, &procErr); /* Activate operation OP2 */ uinstdel (hIn, 0); /* Destroy instance */ unifree (hOp1, pMsg); /* Free the string data */ ufreeh (&hOp1); /* Free OP1 operation handle */ ufreeh (&hOp2); /* Free OP2 operation handle */ ufreeh (&hIn); /* Free instance handle */ ufreeh (&hEnv); /* Free environment handle */ } Copy Related Links Call-In API Return Codes Codes that can possibly be returned by functions from the 3GL call-in API are consistent with the values returned by the ProcScript function $procerror, unless noted otherwise. uprmputs Fill a basic parameter or entity field with Uniface-formatted data. uuf2str Get a pointer to the string data in a basic parameter or entity field.
